title: limonium vulgare subsp. humile
intro: Limonium vulgare subsp. humile, also known as low sea lavender or dwarf sea lavender, is a low-growing, flowering plant species belonging to the Plumbaginaceae family. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties. It is believed to have health benefits such as reducing anxiety and stress, promoting relaxation, and aiding in the treatment of respiratory issues, wounds, and skin conditions. The herbal preparations of Limonium vulgare subsp. humile include infusions, teas, and essential oils, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
